questions.question: The course gives three linked reasons why reporting accidents and incidents exists. Which set correctly lists them?
options: Secrecy, privacy and reputation
Safety, learning and legal duty
Blame, punishment and discipline
Speed, cost and convenience

questions.question: Which piece of law places a general duty on employers to protect the health, safety and welfare of workers and others affected by the work?
options: RIDDOR 2013
UK GDPR
The Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974
CQC Regulation 20 (duty of candour)

questions.question: Under RIDDOR 2013, what does an 'over-seven-day injury' actually describe?
options: A worker who is kept in hospital for exactly seven days
A worker who cannot do their normal duties for more than seven consecutive days
A worker who dies within seven days of the accident
A worker who takes seven days of annual leave after an accident

questions.question: Which of the following is listed as a 'specified injury' under RIDDOR 2013?
options: A fracture to a finger
A minor bruise to the arm
A common cold
An amputation

questions.question: For the most serious cases such as a death, how does RIDDOR require the initial notification to be made?
options: In writing within fifteen days of the event
Without delay, by the quickest means such as a phone call
Within three years of the event
Only within the provider's annual report

questions.question: For how long must records of RIDDOR-reportable events be kept?
options: At least six months
At least ten days
For no set period
At least three years

questions.question: When is a reportable occupational disease reported under RIDDOR?
options: Occupational diseases are never reportable under RIDDOR
Only once the worker has fully recovered
As soon as the responsible person receives a written diagnosis
Within fifteen days of the worker first feeling unwell

questions.question: Under the duty of candour, how and when must the person affected by a notifiable safety incident be told what happened?
options: In person, as soon as reasonably practicable
Only if they specifically ask about it
Through the provider's solicitor
By letter, no sooner than ten days afterwards

questions.question: According to the course, saying sorry to the person affected under the duty of candour is best described as:
options: Something that should always be avoided
The right thing to do, and not an admission of legal liability
An automatic admission of legal liability
A task only a lawyer is allowed to carry out

questions.question: What is a body map used for when recording an incident?
options: To mark the site, size and appearance of any injury
To record staff shift patterns for the week
To plot the building's fire exits and escape routes
To track medication stock levels

questions.question: If you make a mistake while completing a paper incident record, what should you do?
options: Leave the mistake and finish the record next shift
Cover it over completely with correction fluid
Tear the page out and dispose of it
Cross it out with a single line, initial it, and write the correction alongside

questions.question: Which of these entries is a factual statement suitable for an incident report?
options: "Mr Ali is always so clumsy at night"
"It was probably the night staff's fault"
"I found Mr Ali sitting on the floor beside his bed"
"Mr Ali fell because he was being careless"

questions.question: After a serious incident, what should a good investigation look for first and foremost?
options: The root cause — the underlying reason it happened
Grounds to discipline the staff involved
The quickest way to close the file
A single person who can be blamed

questions.question: In the example, Mrs Okafor was given another resident's tablets. What change did the service make after investigating?
options: Dismissed the member of staff who made the error
Added photographs to the medication records and calmed the morning routine
Stopped keeping medication records altogether
Banned all medication from being given at breakfast

questions.question: A service that records a large number of near misses is usually more dangerous than one that records very few.
options: True
False

questions.question: Where a person's death is unexpected, unexplained or may be linked to the care given, it can also have to be reported to the coroner.
options: True
False

questions.question: If equipment failed or a substance was involved, you should not clean up or repair anything until a senior has seen it.
options: True
False

questions.question: If someone is seriously injured or unresponsive, you should wait for your manager's permission before calling 999.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of these are listed as common types of incident in adult social care? (Select all that apply)
options: Safeguarding incidents
Medication errors
A scheduled staff lunch break
Missed or late visits
A planned team handover meeting

questions.question: When something goes wrong, which actions come first, before dealing with the paperwork? (Select all that apply)
options: Check whether anyone is hurt
Complete the incident form before checking on anyone
Stay calm and make the immediate area safe
Repair any broken equipment straight away
Give first aid within the limits of your training

questions.question: When a notifiable safety incident occurs, what does the duty of candour require the service to do? (Select all that apply)
options: Explain what will be done next and offer reasonable support
Give a truthful, step-by-step account of what happened
Conceal the mistake so the person is not worried
Apologise to the person affected
Publicly blame the individual worker involved

questions.question: The course says most incidents come from a chain of factors, rather than one careless person. Which factors does it list? (Select all that apply)
options: The weather outside the building
Staffing
Equipment and procedures
One careless individual to blame
Training

questions.question: A near ___ is an event that could have caused harm but, by luck or quick action, did not.
The duty of candour is set out in CQC Regulation ___.
Records of RIDDOR-reportable events must be kept for at least ___ years.
